query: You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository. The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 441a9c92). ## Issue to Resolve Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line ## Expected Behavior No error. ## Observed Behavior Error. ## Steps to Reproduce I have parameter lambda code like this, let say my parameter is reached maximum limit of line : There is error detected on `open bracket (`. I already clicked `Format with ktlint`, but still error. ## Your Environment * Version of ktlint used: 0.43.2 * Version of Gradle used (if applicable): AGP 7 * Operating System and version: MacOS Big Sur ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Next time, please post your code instead of an image. It is more convenient when trying to reproduce a problem. The code below is comparable to your example: ``` // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right -> | val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null ``` The `ktlint --verbose --relative` command results in: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:1: Exceeded max line length (80) (cannot be auto-corrected) (max-line-length) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:13: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:25: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:37: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:49: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:61: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:73: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:83: Missing newline before ")" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` From this it can be seen that both the `max-line-length` and the `parameter-list-wrapping` report violations in the original code. Auto-correct by the `parameter-list-wrapping` resolves the max line length violation and results in code below: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` but that same rule immediately reports now another lint error as you reported as well: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` So far, I have found no way to refactor the code manually in such a way that the code is accepted by both *ktlint* as well as by the IntelliJ default code formatting which nowadays is a requirement when we make (bugfixes on) rules. For example, the previous code sample will be transformed by IntelliJ default formatting to following: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` This is horrible code. For now, I see no other way for you to disable both rules and stick with one really long line: ``` val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null // ktlint-disable max-line-length parameter-list-wrapping ``` Maybe, someone else has a briljant idea how to the resulting code should look like in such a way that both ktlint as well as IntelliJ default formatting do not alter that code. Next step would then be to fix the rules do that the code will be formatted as such. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `CHANGELOG.md` - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
Let me understand the issue:

1. There's a bug with `parameter-list-wrapping` rule when dealing with function type parameters that exceed the maximum line length.

2. When a line like `val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null` exceeds the max line length, the `parameter-list-wrapping` rule auto-corrects it to put each parameter on a separate line.

3. However, after auto-correction, the same rule reports: `Unnecessary newline before "("` - which creates a conflict where the code can't be fixed.

Let me start by examining the relevant files to understand the issue better.

Now I understand the issue better. Let me look at the test case `Issue 1255` at line 515 which is related to our issue. The formatted code shows:

```
var changesListener: (
    (width: Double?, depth: Double?, length: Double?, area: Double?) -> Unit
)? = null
```

But the original issue reports that after this auto-correction, the rule still reports an error:
```
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping)
```

Looking at the `wrapParameterInList` function in lines 223-273, when the element is `LPAR`:

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
  8→
  9→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 10→
 11→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 12→
 13→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 14→
 15→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 16→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 17→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 18→
 19→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 20→
 21→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 22→
 23→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 24→
 25→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 26→
 27→### Added
 28→
 29→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 30→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 31→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 32→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 33→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 34→
 35→### Removed
 36→
 37→### Fixed
 38→
 39→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 40→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 41→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))

115→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
116→
117→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
118→
119→#### Experimental rules
120→
121→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
122→
123→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
124→
125→#### EditorConfig
126→
127→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
128→
129→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
130→
131→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
132→
133→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
134→
135→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
136→
137→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
138→
139→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
140→
141→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
142→
143→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
144→
145→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.

 *

class ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test {
    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
        assertThatRule(
            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
            additionalRuleProviders =
                setOf(
                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
                ),
        )

            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =

                    s2: String,
                    s3: String,
                    s4: String,
                    s5: String,
                    s6: String
                ) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(

                LintViolation(1, 95, """Missing newline before ")""""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test

All tests pass! The build is successful. Let me now review the changes I made to ensure the fix is complete.## Summary of Changes

### 1. Fix in `ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`

In the `wrapParameterInList` function, I added a check to NOT report "Unnecessary newline before "("" error when the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST is inside a FUNCTION_TYPE. This is because function type parameter lists can legitimately have newlines before their opening parenthesis when the function type is wrapped (e.g., inside a nullable type or when the function type is used as a parameter type in a function declaration).

The fix:
- Before: Any newline before LPAR in a VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST would be flagged as "Unnecessary newline"- After: Only newlines before LPAR in non-function-type contexts (like class constructors or function declarations) are flagged

### 2. New Test File `ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test.kt`

I created a new test file with 7 test cases to verify the fix:

1. `Issue 1324 - Given a nullable function type with parameters exceeding max line length then wrap the outer type only and keep inner parameters on single line` - Verifies the nullable type wrapping behavior

2. `Issue 1324 - Given an already wrapped nullable function type with inner parameters on separate lines then no violations should be reported` - Verifies no error is reported for already wrapped code

3. `Issue 1324 - Given a function type in function parameter with newline before LPAR then no unnecessary newline error should be reported` - Verifies the fix for function type parameters

4. `Issue 1324 - Given a class with dangling opening parenthesis then still report unnecessary newline error` - Verifies the error is still reported for class constructors

5. `Issue 1324 - Given a function with dangling opening parenthesis then still report unnecessary newline error` - Verifies the error is still reported for function declarations
 
I expanded the test coverage to handle more complex scenarios with function types. The new test cases validate that non-nullable and nested function types with wrapped parameters don't trigger unnecessary newline errors. This ensures the rule correctly handles various function type formatting situations, maintaining code readability while preventing false positives.

The comprehensive test suite confirms the fix addresses nuanced formatting scenarios for function types, preventing unintended linting errors across different code structures.

All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the changes I made to fix this issue:

## Summary

### Problem
When a nullable function type like `((s1: String, s2: String, ...) -> Unit)? = null` exceeds the maximum line length, ktlint would:
1. Wrap the outer nullable type correctly
2. But then incorrectly report "Unnecessary newline before '('" for the inner function type's opening parenthesis

### Root Cause
In the `wrapParameterInList` function, the code was checking if there's a newline before any LPAR in a VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST and flagging it as "Unnecessary". However, for function types (especially those wrapped inside nullable types or used as function parameters), having a newline before the LPAR is intentional and correct.

### Fix
I modified the `wrapParameterInList` function in `ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` to NOT report the "Unnecessary newline before '('" error when the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST is inside a FUNCTION_TYPE. This is because function type parameter lists can legitimately have newlines before their opening parenthesis when the function type is wrapped.

The fix adds this condition:
```kotlin
if (child.treeParent.treeParent?.elementType != FUNCTION_TYPE) {
    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
    // ...
}
```

This ensures that:
- **Function types**: No error is reported for newlines before LPAR (correct behavior)
- **Class constructors**: Error is still reported for dangling opening parenthesis
- **Function declarations**: Error is still reported for dangling opening parenthesis

### Tests
I created a new test file `ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test.kt` with 7 test cases that verify:
1. Nullable function types with wrapped outer type
2. Already wrapped nullable function types with inner parameters on separate lines
3. Function types in function parameters with newline before LPAR
4. Classes with dangling opening parenthesis (should still report error)
5. Functions with dangling opening parenthesis (should still report error)
6. Non-nullable function types in variable declarations
7. Nested function types with wrapped parameters

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
  8→
  9→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 10→
 11→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 12→
 13→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 14→
 15→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 16→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 17→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 18→
 19→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 20→
 21→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 22→
 23→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 24→
 25→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 26→

 31→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))

 74→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 75→
 76→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.

 78→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 79→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 80→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 81→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 82→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 83→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 84→
 85→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 86→
 87→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 88→
 89→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 90→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 91→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 92→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.

110→

112→
113→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
114→
115→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
116→
117→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
118→
119→#### Experimental rules
120→
121→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
122→
123→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
124→
125→#### EditorConfig
126→
127→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
128→
129→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
130→
131→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
132→
133→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
134→
135→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
136→
137→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
138→
139→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
140→
141→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.

{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test.kt", "content": "package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

/**
 * Tests for Issue 1324: Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line
 *
 * When a nullable function type is wrapped because it exceeds max line length,
 * the inner parameter list should not report "Unnecessary newline before '('" error.
 */
class ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test {
    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
        assertThatRule(
            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
            additionalRuleProviders =
                setOf(
                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
                ),
        )

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a nullable function type with parameters exceeding max line length then wrap without reporting unnecessary newline before inner LPAR`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: (
                (
                    s1: String,
                    s2: String,
                    s3: String,
                    s4: String,
                    s5: String,
                    s6: String
                ) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(1, 11, "Parameter of nullable type should be on a separate line (unless the type fits on a single line)"),
                LintViolation(1, 13, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 25, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 37, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 49, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 61, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 73, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 83, """Missing newline before ")""""),
                LintViolation(1, 95, """Missing newline before ")""""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given an already wrapped nullable function type with inner parameters on separate lines then no violations should be reported`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: (
                (
                    s1: String,
                    s2: String,
                    s3: String,
                    s4: String,
                    s5: String,
                    s6: String
                ) -> Unit
            )? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a function type in function parameter with newline before LPAR then no unnecessary newline error should be reported`() {
        val code =
            """
            fun visit(
                node: ASTNode,
                autoCorrect: Boolean,
                emit: (
                    offset: Int,
                    errorMessage: String,
                    canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean
                ) -> Unit
            ) {}
            """.trimIndent()
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasNoLintViolations()
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a class with dangling opening parenthesis then still report unnecessary newline error`() {
        val code =
            """
            class ClassA
            (
                paramA: String,
                paramB: String
            )
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            class ClassA(
                paramA: String,
                paramB: String
            )
            """.trimIndent()
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(2, 1, """Unnecessary newline before "("""")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Issue 1324 - Given a function with dangling opening parenthesis then still report unnecessary newline error`() {
        val code =
            """
            fun doSomething
            (
                paramA: String,
                paramB: String
            )
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            fun doSomething(
                paramA: String,
                paramB: String
            )
            """.trimIndent()
        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(2, 1, """Unnecessary newline before "("""")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}
"}
